findings.finding: Task 4's drift test reads predicate_3step_baseline.json as ground truth with no provenance check (no embedded git SHA of resolve_item.py at capture time, no content hash). This leaves the drift detector unable to distinguish a pre-mutation baseline from a post-mutation one.
findings.reviewer_angle: Task sequencing enforcement vs procedural ordering
findings.evidence_quote: Add `test_no_order_drift_against_baseline` that loads `tests/fixtures/predicate_3step_baseline.json`, iterates each `(input, expected_exit, expected_filename)` triple, runs the input through the post-5-step resolver via `_run_live`, and asserts the outcome matches.

findings.finding: Acceptance #4 only checks skill-file contents in this repo. No Acceptance item gates on PR-description content (where the Risks bullet says the skew 'must' be called out), on a version-pinning relationship between CLI wheel and plugin, or on a release-note artifact documenting the recommended update order.
findings.reviewer_angle: Cross-channel skew window for the exit-2 contract
findings.evidence_quote: grep -cE "exit[ -]?2|ambiguous|disambiguat" skills/lifecycle/references/clarify.md skills/refine/SKILL.md skills/lifecycle/references/complete.md skills/lifecycle/references/wontfix.md skills/lifecycle/references/backlog-writeback.md skills/morning-review/SKILL.md` returns ≥6.
